frivolous_filings.basis: MCR 2.114(D) — filing not well-grounded in fact
frivolous_filings.description: PPO petition contained false allegations of physical violence. Andrew Pigors has no violence convictions. Straw-throwing incident (Oct 2023) fabricated or grossly exaggerated to obtain emergency protection order.

frivolous_filings.basis: MCR 2.114(D) — interposed for improper purpose (harassment, delay)
frivolous_filings.description: Serial false allegations used as litigation weapon to deprive Andrew of parental rights. Pattern of filing motions timed to interfere with parenting time.

frivolous_filings.basis: MCR 2.114(D), MCL 722.27a(3) — no required emergency findings
frivolous_filings.description: August 2025 ex parte order suspending ALL parenting time without the required statutory findings under MCL 722.27a(3). No evidence of imminent danger to child.

frivolous_filings.basis: MCR 2.114(A) — unsigned filings by non-attorney, MCL 600.916 UPL
frivolous_filings.description: Ronald Berry, a non-attorney, drafted, edited, or prepared legal documents filed in this case. These filings violate MCR 2.114 signing requirements and constitute unauthorized practice of law.

discovery_abuse.basis: MCR 2.313(B)(2)(a) — failure to comply with discovery
discovery_abuse.description: Failure to produce medical records for L.D.W., financial records, and communications with Ronald Berry as required by discovery requests.
discovery_abuse.remedy: Order compelling production + costs of motion

discovery_abuse.description: Provided incomplete and evasive responses regarding Ronald Berry's role, withholding periods, and communications with FOC.
discovery_abuse.remedy: Treat matters as admitted per MCR 2.313(B)(2)(c)

attorney_fees_equivalent.description: Pro se litigant hourly rate for time spent responding to frivolous filings
attorney_fees_equivalent.authority: MCL 600.2591(2) — costs include reasonable attorney fees

filing_fees.description: Filing fees incurred responding to frivolous motions

travel_costs.description: Travel to and from court for unnecessary hearings

lost_parenting_time.description: 268 documented days of lost parenting time due to frivolous filings and false allegations

emotional_distress.description: Emotional distress from false allegations, parental alienation, and separation from child

authorities.rule: Sanctions for frivolous filings — court SHALL award costs and fees
Sanctions against represented party AND attorney jointly
Sanctions for discovery abuse — compelling, striking, default
Costs for frivolous civil actions or defenses
Unauthorized practice of law — criminal penalty
Federal civil rights — compensatory + punitive damages
Court's inherent power to sanction bad-faith litigation conduct

authorities.cite: Contel Sys v Appolonia 169 Mich App 484 (1988)

authorities.rule: MCL 600.2591 — 'frivolous' includes no reasonable basis in fact or law

sections.heading: I. INTRODUCTION
sections.content: Plaintiff Andrew James Pigors moves this Court for sanctions against Defendant Emily A. Watson and non-party Ronald T. Berry pursuant to MCR 2.114(D)-(E), MCR 2.313(B), MCL 600.2591, and this Court's inherent authority.

sections.heading: II. STATEMENT OF FACTS
sections.content: The pattern of sanctionable conduct spans from 2023 to present and includes: (a) filing a PPO petition based on false allegations; (b) serial custody interference totaling 268 documented days; (c) unauthorized practice of law by Ronald Berry; (d) procurement of an ex parte order without statutory findings; (e) discovery abuse through evasion and non-production.

sections.heading: III. ARGUMENT
subsections: A. Sanctions Are Mandatory Under MCR 2.114(E)
D. Ronald Berry's UPL Warrants Referral Under MCL 600.916
E. The Court's Inherent Authority Supports Additional Sanctions

sections.heading: V. RELIEF REQUESTED
items: Award of costs and fees under MCR 2.114(E) and MCL 600.2591
Order compelling discovery responses under MCR 2.313
Referral of Ronald Berry to State Bar for UPL investigation
Default on discovery matters per MCR 2.313(B)(2)(c)
Such other relief as this Court deems just and proper
